# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create a temporary file with a predictable name
TMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$(uuidgen).tmp"

# Write some sample data to the temp file
echo "Sample data" > "$TMP_FILE"

# Process the temp file (e.g. compress it)
gzip -9 "$TMP_FILE"

# Read results from the compressed temp file
cat <(gunzip -c "$TMP_FILE.gz")  # Note: File name changed due to gzip addition

# Example of using the same temp file path multiple times in the script
gzip -9 "$TMP_FILE" # Compress again
cat <(gunzip -c "$TMP_FILE.gz")
